caption: Axial view brain MRI showing peripheral enhancing lesion with an unchanged size (red arrow) and with decreased size of vasogenic edema (yellow arrow).
Magnetic resonance imaging. Gadolinium-enhanced axial T1 image showing a meningeal enhancement at the left parietal lobe
Axial view computed tomography angiogram (CTA) image of the carotid arteries showing a thrombus in the right common carotid artery extending into carotid bifurcation.

MRI-guided localization. Axial, T1W non– fat-suppressed MRI image shows a thin magnetic susceptibility artifact (arrow) due to the localization wire in place. The lesion (arrowhead) is seen near the tip
Axial brain MRI showing decrease in size of cystic mass (red arrow).
Magnetic resonance imaging. Contrast enhanced Axial T1 image showing a temporoparietal hypointense area with few petechial lesions
CT scan with sagittal view of inferior vena cava thrombus
Preoperative Axial T2 of the skull base revealed multiple fluid-fluid levels with hemorrhagic areas (white arrow) are consistent with associated aneurysmal bone cyst.
Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) coronal slices showing bilateral subchondral geodes and thinned cortical of mandibular condylar process
Sagittal T2-weighted magnetic resonance imaging shows diffuse bone marrow signal change, endplate destruction and epidural abscess formation at the L2–3 level.
